alkylation reagent: addition in the medium of iodoacetamide and of the desubiquitinase inhibitor PR619.
alkylation concentration: alkylation with 55 mM iodoacetamide in 25 mM NH4HCO3
cleavage agent: in-gel digestion with trypsin using a Freedom EVO150 robotic platform
collision energy: the 10 most intense ions were fragmented by higher-energy collisional dissociation (nce = 30)
enrichment method: purified from 10-day plantlets by GFP-affinity and analyzed by Western blot
fragmentation method: the 10 most intense ions were fragmented by higher-energy collisional dissociation
instrument: analyzed via online nano-LC–MS/MS (Ultimate 3000 RSLCnano and Q-Exactive Plus, Thermo Fisher Scientific)
ionization type: equipped with a Jet stream electrospray ion source
labeling: peptides were analyzed via online nano-LC–MS/MS
reduction reagent: reduction was carried out with 10 mM DTT in 25 mM NH4HCO3
reduction concentration: reduction was carried out with 10 mM DTT in 25 mM NH4HCO3
